consistency.casting_time_and_duration: Consistent. 1 minute casting time results in an instantaneous effect, which aligns with the purpose of receiving a single piece of information (an omen).
consistency.components: Consistent. The material component (M) has a specified cost (25 gp) and description, indicating it is consumed or required for the ritualistic nature of the spell.

quality.flavor_and_theme: High quality. The description clearly ties the mechanical effect (receiving an omen) to various thematic methods of divination (sticks, bones, cards).
quality.clarity_of_effect: High clarity. The four possible omens are explicitly listed and defined.
quality.limitations: Good clarity on limitations. It explicitly states the omen is for a specific course of action within 30 minutes and ignores future variables (like other spells or companions), which is crucial for mechanical balance.

mechanics.action_economy: Casting time of 1 minute is standard for non-combat ritual spells, fitting for gathering information.
mechanics.output_utility: The output ('Weal', 'Woe', etc.) provides directional guidance but leaves the interpretation and planning to the player, offering limited but useful utility without guaranteeing success.
mechanics.resource_cost: Material component cost (25 gp) is appropriate for a level 2 divination spell, especially one that is a ritual.
mechanics.recursion_penalty: The penalty for casting the spell multiple times (cumulative 25% chance of a random reading per extra casting) is a well-designed soft cap on repeated use, preventing players from fishing for the best possible omen by spamming the spell.
